Strategy Name: Combining Fundamental FSCORE and Equity Short-Term Reversals
Strategy Description: Enhances the traditional short-term reversal strategy by using fundamental strength (FSCORE) to filter for higher-probability reversals.
Core Logic: Short-term reversals are driven by noise trading and investor underreaction to fundamental news. Buying losers with strong fundamentals exploits this underreaction.
Entry Conditions: Long past 1-month losers with an FSCORE >= 7; short past 1-month winners with an FSCORE <= 3.
Indicators Used: Past 1-month returns, FSCORE (9 signals covering profitability, leverage, and efficiency).
Risk Management Rules: Equally weighted; focus on large stocks (top 40% of market capitalization).
Market Regime Suitability: Performs well during market crises as a 'liquidity providing' strategy; however, it is naturally 'short volatility'.
Full Notes: FSCORE is a nonparametric metric that synthesizes information from nine financial signals. The strategy outperforms unconditional reversal.
